_instructions.overview: This directory contains the encounter database for Audit Quest: Global Compliance. Each facility has its own JSON file containing an array of encounter objects. The game loads these files at runtime — edit them and reload the browser to see changes.
how_to_add_encounters: 2. Find a PLACEHOLDER entry or copy the template below
3. Replace ALL placeholder values with real content from Enhesa audit scorecards
4. Ensure is_noncompliance is: true, false, or "ambiguous"
5. Ensure correct_category is one of the 8 valid categories listed below
6. Ensure distractor_categories has exactly 3 OTHER valid categories
7. Save the file and reload the game — no rebuild needed
_instructions.id_format: XX-YYY-NNN where XX=country code, YYY=facility abbreviation, NNN=number (e.g., UK-OFF-001, DE-MFG-012)

content_guidelines.mix: Aim for ~65% non-compliance, ~25% compliant (answer: No), ~10% ambiguous (answer: Need More Info)
content_guidelines.difficulty_scale: 1 = obvious violations, 2 = moderate, 3 = subtle, 4 = edge cases, 5 = expert-level
content_guidelines.scene_description: 2-3 sentences describing what the player SEES. Be specific and visual. Don't reveal the answer.
content_guidelines.explanation: 2-4 sentences. State the correct answer, explain WHY referencing the regulation, connect to the category, use accessible language for new hires.
content_guidelines.humor: Occasional funny or absurd situations are encouraged while keeping the compliance content accurate.
